title: lepigonum fallax
side_effects: Lepigonum fallax can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness, especially when ingested in large quantities or without proper medical supervision. Some individuals have also reported experiencing headaches, stomach cramps, and diarrhea after using the plant for medicinal purposes without following recommended dosages. Furthermore, Lepigonum fallax may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and exacerbate underlying health conditions like high blood pressure and kidney disease. In severe cases, improper use of Lepigonum fallax can lead to more serious complications, including allergic reactions and respiratory issues.
